docs.title: 🏠 Welcome to my Labwc Config
docs.text: A lightweight, Openbox-style stacking Wayland compositor setup on Arch Linux. This configuration embraces the classic floating paradigm with powerful edge-snapping, a classic Clearlooks theme, and a highly customized Waybar featuring pop-out drawers for power and battery options.

docs.title: ✨ Highlights
docs.text:
• Classic Aesthetics: Uses the Clearlooks-3.4 theme with 8px rounded corners.
  • Waybar Drawers: Grouped power and battery modules that expand on hover for a clean UI.
  • Theme Switcher: Click the backlight icon in Waybar to switch themes on the fly.
  • Openbox Familiarity: XML configuration (rc.xml, menu.xml) with standard keyboard/mouse bindings.

docs.title: 📸 Gallery
docs.text: Here are various screenshots of the Labwc setup in action.

docs.title: 🛠️ Installation
docs.text: This configuration uses standard Labwc XDG base directories and a custom Waybar launched via the autostart script.

docs.title: Prerequisites
docs.text:
Core: - labwc - waybar - rofi - swaybg (Wallpaper setter) - swaync (Notification center)

Utilities: - lxterminal (Default terminal) - brightnessctl (Backlight) - amixer / alsa-utils (Volume control)

docs.title: Deploying the Config
docs.text:
1. Clone the repository:

    git clone https://codeberg.org/WgpArch/labwc.git ~/.dotfiles/labwc

  2. Symlink the Labwc configuration:

    ln -sf ~/.dotfiles/labwc/configs/labwc ~/.config/labwc

  3. Ensure scripts are executable:

    chmod +x ~/.config/labwc/scripts/*
chmod +x ~/.config/labwc/waybar/scripts/*

docs.title: ⌨️ Keybinds
docs.text: Labwc uses standard Openbox-style XML keybindings. The main modifier is W (Super/Windows key).

docs.title: 🖥️ Programs & System
docs.text: Keybind Action W + Return Open Terminal (lxterminal) W + Q Close focused window W + Up Maximize/Restore window W + Down Minimize (Iconify) window W + Print Take screenshot (Custom script) A + F4 Close window (Classic binding)

docs.title: 🔍 Focus & Window Switching
docs.text: Keybind Action A + Tab Next Window A + Shift + Tab Previous Window

docs.title: 📐 Snapping & Movement
docs.text: Keybind Action W + Left Snap to Left Half W + Right Snap to Right Half W + Up Snap to Top Half / Maximize W + Down Snap to Bottom Half / Minimize W + A Toggle Maximize

docs.title: 🖱️ Mouse Bindings
docs.text: Action Binding W + Left Click Drag Move Window W + Right Click Drag Resize Window Desktop Right Click Open Root Menu Titlebar Right Click Open Client Menu

docs.title: 🔊 Media Keys
docs.text: Keybind Action Volume Up/Down Master Volume 5% +/- (amixer) Mute Toggle Master Mute Brightness Up/Down Backlight 10% +/- (brightnessctl)

docs.title: 🎨 Look & Feel
docs.text: This Labwc config embraces a classic, familiar desktop aesthetic, styled closely after traditional Openbox.

docs.title: General Appearance
docs.text:
• Decoration: Server-side decorations (titlebars) enabled.
  • Theme: Clearlooks-3.4 (Classic GTK look).
  • Corner Radius: 8px
  • Font: Sans, size 10.
  • Drop Shadows: Disabled for a clean, flat look.

docs.title: Window Snapping
docs.text:
• Top Maximize: Dragging a window to the top edge will maximize it.
  • Edge Snapping: Dragging to left/right edges snaps to 50%.
  • Resistance: 20px resistance at screen and window edges before snapping.

docs.title: Window Switcher (Alt-Tab)
docs.text:
• Style: Classic list format.
  • Preview: Enabled (shows window outlines).
  • Fields: Displays the application icon, desktop entry name, and window title.

docs.title: Autostart
docs.text: Applications are spawned automatically via ~/.config/labwc/autostart: - Wallpaper: swaybg sets a dark road wallpaper. - Waybar: Launches with config.jsonc and style.css. - Network: nm-applet - Notifications: swaync

docs.title: Input Devices
docs.text:
• Keyboard: Repeat rate 25, delay 600.
  • Touchpad: Tap-to-click enabled. Scroll factor 1.0.

docs.title: 📊 Waybar & Scripts
docs.text: This setup uses a custom Waybar positioned at the bottom, featuring grouped "drawer" modules that expand on hover to save space.

docs.title: Left
docs.text:
• Custom Arch Launcher: Opens Rofi (custom config.rasi).
  • WLR Workspaces: Displays 11 workspaces with Nerd Font icons (Terminal, Browser, Files, Code, Games, Chat, Video, Music, Headphones, Users, Settings).
  • WLR Taskbar: Shows currently open windows. Left-click to minimize, middle-click to close, right-click to maximize.

docs.title: Center
docs.text:
• Clock: Date, time, and calendar tooltip.
  • Custom Weather: Fetches weather data via a custom Ruby script (get_weather.rb).

docs.title: Right
docs.text:
• Tray, CPU, Memory, Temperature: Standard system monitors.
  • Custom Notification: SwayNC integration.
  • Pulseaudio: Volume control.
  • Backlight: Brightness percentage. Click to switch Waybar themes! (switch_themes.sh)
  • Network: WiFi signal strength and IP info.
  • Battery Drawer: Hover to reveal Power Profiles Daemon toggle.
  • Logout Drawer: Hover to reveal Power, Lock, Reboot, Logout, and Sleep buttons.

docs.title: 📜 Key Scripts
docs.text:
Located in ~/.config/labwc/scripts/:

Script Description screenshot_labwc.sh Screenshot utility. logout.sh Cleanly exits the Labwc session.

Located in ~/.config/labwc/waybar/scripts/:

Script Description switch_themes.sh Toggles Waybar CSS themes on the fly. get_weather.sh / weather.py Weather data fetchers.
